description:
You are an operations engineer who has just been paged to perform a very quick reachability
    check against the loopback interface of the host that the incident-response
    container is running on.  
    1. A single IPv4 address has already been placed in the file  
       /home/user/target_ip.txt  
       The file contains exactly one line and **nothing else**: “127.0.0.1”.  
    2. Your goal is to prove that the host can reach that address and to capture
       the result in a one-line log file for the on-call report.  
       
    Required output artefact
    ------------------------
    Create a new directory (if it does not already exist):  
        /home/user/logs

    Inside that directory create a text file named:  
        /home/user/logs/ping_summary.log

    The file must contain exactly **one line** (no leading/trailing blank lines).  
    That line must be the packet-loss summary line produced by the standard
    Linux `ping` utility after sending **exactly three (3)** echo requests
    to the target IP address.

    Format specification that the automated grader will check:  
      • The line must begin with the text:  
            3 packets transmitted  
      • The line must also contain the substring (anywhere on the same line):  
            0% packet loss  
      • The grader will ignore everything after the phrase “0% packet loss”.

    Example of an acceptable line (values after “time” may differ on your run):

        3 packets transmitted, 3 received, 0% packet loss, time 2043ms

    There must be **no other content** in /home/user/logs/ping_summary.log.

    Rules & constraints
    -------------------
    • You may use any standard GNU/Linux command-line tools available in the
      container.  
    • Do **not** hard-code the summary line; the agent must actually run the
      diagnostic and capture its real output.  
    • Do not require root privileges.  
    • Do not modify /home/user/target_ip.txt.  
    • Place all working or temporary files only inside /home/user or /tmp.  

    Once the file /home/user/logs/ping_summary.log exists and meets the
    format above, the task is complete.
truth:
<!-- The testing harness will verify that the file below exists and
         contains exactly one line that starts with the required phrase and
         contains “0% packet loss”. -->
    expected_log_path: /home/user/logs/ping_summary.log
    expected_line_prefix: "3 packets transmitted"
    expected_substring: "0% packet loss"
    # No timing information is checked because it can vary.
name: task_000000_bd088bc1
